    Coverage Growth Timeline

    Coverage Growth — Cities Confirmed Over Time

    New DMAs Unlocked Per Day (by Tier)

    Signal Velocity (Signals Per Day)

    Multi-day history needed for velocity.
    Today shown as baseline bar.

    Methodology

    This tracker maps Amazon Same-Day perishable grocery delivery coverage across the United States. The following documents our data sources, probe strategies, confidence model, and known limitations.

    1. What We're Tracking

    In December 2025, Amazon announced that Same-Day Delivery for perishable groceries — fresh produce, meat, dairy, and other perishables — was available in over 2,300 US cities and towns for Prime members, with further expansion planned for 2026. Amazon does not publish a list of covered cities or zip codes; availability must be checked individually at the zip code level.

    This tracker attempts to map that coverage by assembling signals from multiple sources and building city- and DMA-level confidence scores.

    2. Geographic Framework

    We organize coverage around three tiers of geography:

    • City/town — the unit Amazon uses in its announcement ("2,300 cities and towns"). Our city table contains ~29,700 named places mapped to zip codes.
    • DMA (Designated Market Area) — the 210 Nielsen television markets, used as the primary analytical unit. DMAs reflect real economic and logistics catchment areas far better than municipal boundaries. Omaha, for example, is DMA #73 and includes Council Bluffs IA, Papillion, Bellevue, Elkhorn, and dozens of other communities.
    • Zip code — the ground-truth unit Amazon actually checks. Used for validation and for mapping city/DMA assignments.

    All 31,913 US zip codes are mapped to their DMA via a spatial centroid crosswalk (zip centroid plotted against DMA boundary polygons). Cities inherit their DMA from the plurality DMA of their zip codes.

    3. Amazon's Same-Day Delivery Facility Network

    Amazon operates a network of Same-Day Delivery (SSD) sites — a mix of purpose-built facilities and retrofitted existing distribution centers — that have been upgraded with temperature-controlled zones and specialized cold-chain handling to support perishable grocery fulfillment. These are distinct from Amazon Fresh dark stores and traditional large fulfillment centers. Amazon describes them as its "specialized temperature-controlled fulfillment network," using the same insulated recyclable bags as Amazon Fresh and Whole Foods Market deliveries. Facilities typically run ~150,000–270,000 sq ft and use Amazon Flex contract drivers for final-mile delivery.

    Multiple Amazon press releases and local news articles have confirmed the service radius at 50 miles for these facilities:

    • Council Bluffs, IA facility (serving Omaha metro): "covers a 50-mile radius" — KETV, Dec 2024
    • Ankeny, IA facility (serving Des Moines): "within 50 miles" — Des Moines Register, Dec 2024

    This means a single facility can serve an entire DMA. When we confirm coverage in a core city, we treat the surrounding 50-mile area as likely covered and probe suburbs accordingly.

    4. Probe Strategy by DMA Tier

    DMAs are tiered by TV household count and probed with different strategies proportional to their complexity:

    TierDMA RanksTV HomesMethodsQueries/DMA
    Mega1–102.5M+News · Plain language · Reddit · Facebook · Zip grid40
    Large11–351M–2.5MNews · Plain language · Reddit · Zip grid15
    Mid36–80400K–1MNews · Plain language6
    Small81–170100K–400KNews only2
    Micro171–210<100KSkipped0

    The probe queue processes DMAs in priority order (highest rank first). As of the current run, 195 queries have been completed and 124 remain queued.

    5. Signal Sources

    Each data point in our signals table is tagged with a source and type:

    SourceSignal TypesConfidence Assigned
    Official announcementConfirmed available90%
    Brave search — newsConfirmed available / unavailable / mention70–75% / 40%
    Brave search — plain language"Can I get same-day fresh groceries from Amazon in [city]"65–75%
    Reddit / socialUser-reported availability60–70%
    DMA inferenceInferred from confirmed neighboring cities within 50mi radius50–60%
    Playwright (manual validation)Direct zip check at amazon.com/grocery95%+

    6. Confidence Model

    Each city-level coverage record carries a confidence score (0–100). Scores are assigned at signal creation and can be upgraded as stronger signals arrive:

    • 50 — Default / single mention with no direct confirmation
    • 60–70 — News article or social post mentioning the city
    • 75 — Brave search result with explicit availability language
    • 90 — Official Amazon press release or announcement
    • 95+ — Direct zip-code check via amazon.com/grocery (Playwright validation)

    DMA-level coverage is a rollup: the percentage of known cities in that DMA with at least one confirmed signal. DMA confidence reflects the average city confidence within the DMA.

    Current average city confidence: 69.9%

    7. Known Limitations

    • Signals are biased toward availability. People post when a service works, rarely when it doesn't. Confirmed unavailability is harder to find and underrepresented.
    • City count vs. zip code coverage. Amazon uses zip codes internally. A city may have partial coverage (some zips yes, some no). Our city-level data is a simplification.
    • DMA coverage % is a lower bound. We have confirmed cities for only a fraction of cities per DMA. The true coverage is almost certainly higher than our current percentage shows — we just haven't probed all cities yet.
    • The 2,300 city figure may already be outdated. Amazon stated expansion would continue in 2026. The real number is likely higher today.
    • Mega-DMA neighborhood granularity. In markets like Los Angeles, coverage varies block-by-block. A DMA-level "covered" designation may mask significant intra-DMA gaps.

    8. Daily Probe Schedule

    The probe pipeline runs automatically at 6:00 AM PT daily:

    1. city_probe.js — Brave search sweep across top metros
    2. dma_probe.js --max 30 — Works 30 queries from the priority queue
    3. snapshot.js — Captures daily rollup for timeline tracking
    4. report.js — Regenerates this HTML report

    Results are delivered via Telegram and email summary. The timeline chart accumulates one data point per day — projections toward completion will become available after approximately one week of data.

    9. Technology Stack

    • Database: SQLite (better-sqlite3) — 31,913 zip codes, 29,700 cities, 210 DMAs, cumulative signals log
    • Search API: Brave Search API — web, news, and social signals
    • Map: Leaflet.js with OpenStreetMap tiles + US county GeoJSON choropleth
    • Charts: Chart.js
    • Runtime: Node.js 22, macOS, OpenClaw AI assistant orchestration

    Research conducted by Rivendell Advisors LLC. Generated Mar 24, 2026, 07:12 AM PDT.
